element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Base ability; fire breath already present as innate power
Specialized for ground combat and wall-climbing; unlocks ground-based passages
Enables flight and aerial exploration; revisits earlier areas with new access paths
Water-based abilities; unlocks aquatic stage elements
Advanced combat form; prepares for final boss encounters
Secret form unlocked after third ending; enables access to hidden fourth ending and Dark Demon boss
Final objective; Phalanx transforms using this against Firebrand
Optional power-ups; collected alongside talismans to augment abilities
Passive improvements to Firebrand's core stats and abilities; found through exploration

typical_relief_triggers: Collecting new Crest or transformation ability
Revisiting previous stage with new powers (Metroidvania progression)
Returning to world map for navigation choice
typical_tension_triggers: Boss encounters with Phalanx's general Arma
Somulo (opening amphitheater battle); doubled HP in NA version
Final Phalanx encounter with multiple ending branches
Stage revisits with new challenges in previously-accessible areas
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Cyclical progression with mandatory replays; tension builds across 6-stage progression toward final confrontation; relief via transformation unlocks and exploration freedom on world map

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Walls blocking spell shop (unused NPC dialogue references inaccessible content); new transformation abilities unlock previously-blocked passages
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Hidden fourth ending and Dark Demon boss unlocked via completing game with third ending and obtaining special password to continue as Ultimate Gargoyle

difficulty_curve.early_game: Moderate difficulty; Firebrand begins wounded and weak; basic platforming with fire breath; Somulo introductory boss (easier in JP version with 3 hits vs NA version with 6 hits)
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalating complexity; multiple Crest-gated areas require revisiting stages; Metroidvania-style backtracking introduces environmental puzzles; Arma encounters increase in difficulty
difficulty_curve.late_game: High difficulty; final castle progression; Phalanx multi-phase battle with three possible endings based on player choice; optional secret fourth ending branch for dedicated players
notable_spikes: Somulo opening battle (significantly harder in North American version)
Phalanx final battle with branching outcomes

notable_level_patterns.description: Six stages divided into main areas; players must collect Crests to unlock new transformations, which are then required to access previously-blocked sections and new bosses. Enforces multiple playthroughs of same levels with evolving capabilities.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Multiple stages; spell shop area in stage II unreachable without proper abilities

notable_level_patterns.description: Each Gargoyle form (Ground, Aerial, Tidal, Legendary) specializes in different movement and combat mechanics. Stages hide optional paths and items requiring specific forms.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Aerial Gargoyle required for high passages; Tidal Gargoyle for water zones

notable_level_patterns.description: Final Phalanx encounter offers three branching endings based on player decisions during battle; choosing path to best ending (third) unlocks Ultimate Gargoyle form and fourth secret ending.
Players navigate six stage areas via world map, providing strategic choice and pacing breaks between action sequences.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All stages accessible via world map hub
